title: Database - Knowledge Level of First Aid
author: Lima, Josivaldo
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Title : Knowledge Level of First Aid UBO 
  Description : This dataset contains survey responses collected to assess the knowledge level of first aid among participants from UBO. The survey includes demographic information, such as gender, age group, education, region of residence, monthly income, marital status, religion, and work experience as a teacher. Additionally, the survey contains questions related to first aid knowledge and procedures, experiences of witnessing accidents requiring first aid, and familiarity with emergency and evacuation plans during earthquakes. The dataset aims to evaluate the overall understanding and preparedness of individuals in handling first aid situations. 
   
  Fields in the Dataset : 
 
  ID : Identifier for each participant. 
  A. Accepts to Participate? : Participant's consent to participate in the survey. 
  B. Gender : Participant's gender. 
  C. Age Group : Age group of the participant. 
  D. Education : Level of education of the participant. 
  E. Region of Residence : Region where the participant lives. 
  F. Monthly Income : Participant's monthly income. 
  G. Marital Status : Participant's marital status. 
  H. Religion : Participant's religion. 
  I. Work Experience as a Teacher : Participant's experience working as a teacher. 
  1. Importance of First Aid Knowledge : Rating (0-10) of the importance of first aid knowledge in personal life. 
  2. Presence During Accident Requiring First Aid : Indication if the participant has witnessed an accident requiring first aid. 
  3. Treating a Deep Cut : Procedure to handle a deep cut. 
  4. Handling Epistaxis : Correct procedure for dealing with a nosebleed. 
  5. First Aid for Burns : Steps for providing first aid to burn victims. 
  6. Procedures for Exposed Bone Fracture : Correct procedures for treating an exposed bone fracture. 
  7. Symptoms of Acute Myocardial Infarction : Recognizing symptoms of a heart attack. 
  8. First Aid for Heart Attack Victims : Procedures to assist a heart attack victim. 
  9. Procedures for Stroke Victims : Basic life support procedures for stroke victims. 
  10. Initial Actions for Suspected Heart Attack : First steps when suspecting a heart attack. 
  11. Actions at an Emergency Scene : Correct actions to take at an emergency scene. 
  12. Obtaining Consent from a Conscious Victim : Steps after obtaining consent from a conscious victim. 
  13. Presuming Shock : Conditions under which shock should be presumed. 
  14. Head Injury Response : Actions when suspecting a head injury. 
  15. Diabetic Confusion Response : Correct response to a confused diabetic during exercise. 
  16. Difficulty Breathing : Signs that indicate breathing difficulty. 
  17. Handling a Suspected Leg Fracture : Procedures for a possible leg fracture. 
  18. First Aid for an Embedded Object : Steps for treating an embedded object in a limb. 
  19. Technique for Choking Victim : Recommended technique for a choking victim. 
  20. Vital Signs and General Condition : Importance of vital signs in assessing general condition. 
  21. Earthquake Emergency Plan Awareness : Knowledge of emergency and evacuation plans for earthquakes. 
  22. Participation in Emergency Drills : Involvement in workplace emergency drills. 
  23. Workplace Risk Assessment : Evaluation of workplace risks in case of an earthquake. 
  24. Knowledge of Evacuation Routes : Awareness of evacuation routes. 
  25. Actions During an Earthquake : Correct actions to take during an earthquake. 
  26. Door Management During an Earthquake : How to manage doors during an earthquake. 
  27. Vehicle Management During an Earthquake : Actions to take if driving during an earthquake. 
  28. Avoiding Equipment Use During an Earthquake : Equipment to avoid using during an earthquake. 
  29. Earthquake Training : Previous training received for earthquake response. 
  30. First Aid Training : Previous training received in first aid.
